summary: request-cache-py exfiltrates SSH/cloud credentials and browser secrets to attacker Telegram bot on import
details: On `import request_cache_py`, the package's top-level code in `request_cache_py/__init__.py` runs a credential-harvest pipeline against the importing user's machine and ships the results to a hardcoded Telegram bot. Specifically it reads `~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`, `~/.ssh/id_ecdsa`, `~/.ssh/id_dsa`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.aws/config`, `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.git-credentials`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.pypirc`, `~/.dockercfg`, `~/.docker/config.json`, gcloud credentials, and VSCode settings; copies Chrome/Edge/Safari `Cookies`, `Login Data`, and `History` SQLite databases to `/tmp` and runs `SELECT host_key, name, value, path FROM cookies` / `SELECT origin_url, username_value FROM logins` to extract session cookies and saved credentials; and scans `os.environ` for any variable whose name contains `key`, `token`, `secret`, `password`, `api`, or `auth`. The collected blob, with hostname/user/cwd, is POSTed to `https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage` (chat id 8276546637); the bot token is base64-obfuscated and split across list parts to evade string scanners. The harvest is gated by sandbox/CI evasion (skips when `/proc/cpuinfo` shows a hypervisor, when `/.dockerenv` exists, or when `CI`/`GITHUB_ACTIONS` are set) and writes a 24h dedup marker at `~/.cache/.pyrc`. The package presents a benign-looking HTTP cache API (`cached_get`, `cached_post`, `MemoryCache`) and a generic 'Python Performance Team' author identity as cover, mimicking legitimate libraries like `requests-cache` / `cachecontrol`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
